No Poverty.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course syllabus links to poverty eradication.

Zero Hunger.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course syllabus links to hunger, food security, or sustainable agriculture.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course syllabus links to health, well-being, or disease prevention.

Quality Education.reason: As a university course, it directly provides tertiary education and skills for employment, aligning with Targets 4.3 and 4.4. Core competence A also supports this.
evidence: 奠定相關基礎專業知識(Students will acquire and apply expertise in their respective fields.)

Gender Equality.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course syllabus links to gender equality or women's empowerment.

Clean Water and Sanitation.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course syllabus links to water management or sanitation.

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: The 'Lean Production' topic aims to reduce waste, which includes energy, thereby contributing to energy efficiency (Target 7.3).

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course focuses on improving economic productivity and resource efficiency through 'Lean Production' and 'Quality Control', directly supporting Targets 8.2 and 8.4. Core competence B also mentions sustainability.

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: Core topics like 'Supply Chain Management' and 'Lean Production' directly support sustainable industrialization and resource-use efficiency in industries (Targets 9.2, 9.4).

Reduced Inequalities.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course syllabus links to reducing inequalities.

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: An inferred link exists as 'Supply Chain Management' relates to transport systems (Target 11.2) and 'Lean Production' to waste reduction (Target 11.6), contributing to urban sustainability.

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: Directly addresses responsible production through 'Lean Production' (waste reduction) and efficient 'Supply Chain Management', aligning with Targets 12.2, 12.5, and 12.6. Core competence B explicitly mentions sustainability.

Climate Action.reason: Efficient 'Supply Chain Management' and 'Lean Production' reduce energy use and waste, indirectly mitigating climate change, supported by the mention of 'sustainability issues' (Target 13.2).

Life Below Water.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course syllabus links to marine ecosystems or ocean health.

Life on Land.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course syllabus links to terrestrial ecosystems or biodiversity.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course syllabus links to peace, justice, or institutional strength.

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: The study of 'Supply Chain Management' inherently involves managing partnerships between different entities, which is foundational for Target 17.17 on multi-stakeholder partnerships.
